parsed.description: server-c1 is currently serving traffic from the configuration loaded at startup, but /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf has been corrupted afterward by the entrypoint script. As a result, nginx configuration validation and reloads fail, preventing safe configuration changes or service reloads and creating operational risk on the next restart/redeploy.
parsed.issue: Nginx continues serving but the active configuration file is deliberately corrupted after startup, making config validation/reload fail. Root cause: server-c1 entrypoint.sh appends an unterminated server block containing an invalid directive to /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf after nginx has started.
parsed.remediation:
Run this on server-c1 as root. It backs up the files, removes entrypoint lines/heredocs that append to /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf, restores a valid default server config, validates it, and reloads Nginx. If server-c1 is container/image-built, also apply this entrypoint change in the image/source repo and redeploy so it persists.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

CONF=/etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
ENTRYPOINT=${ENTRYPOINT:-}
if [[ -z "$ENTRYPOINT" ]]; then
  for p in /entrypoint.sh /usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh /docker-entrypoint.sh /usr/local/bin/docker-entrypoint.sh; do
    [[ -f "$p" ]] && ENTRYPOINT="$p" && break
  done
fi
[[ -f "$ENTRYPOINT" ]] || { echo "Set ENTRYPOINT=/path/to/entrypoint.sh and rerun" >&2; exit 1; }

ts=$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)
cp -a "$ENTRYPOINT" "$ENTRYPOINT.bak.$ts"
cp -a "$CONF" "$CONF.bak.$ts"

tmp=$(mktemp)
skip=0
delim=
while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
  if [[ "$skip" -eq 1 ]]; then
    trimmed=$(printf '%s\n' "$line" | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//;s/[[:space:]]*$//')
    [[ "$trimmed" == "$delim" ]] && skip=0
    continue
  fi

  if [[ "$line" == *"$CONF"* && ( "$line" == *">>"* || "$line" =~ tee[[:space:]]+-a ) ]]; then
    if [[ "$line" == *"<<"* ]]; then
      delim=${line#*<<}
      delim=${delim#-}
      delim=$(printf '%s\n' "$delim" | awk '{print $1}' | tr -d "'\"")
      skip=1
    fi
    continue
  fi

  printf '%s\n' "$line" >> "$tmp"
done < "$ENTRYPOINT"
cat "$tmp" > "$ENTRYPOINT"
rm -f "$tmp"
chmod +x "$ENTRYPOINT"

cat > "$CONF" <<'NGINX'
server {
    listen 80 default_server;
    listen [::]:80 default_server;
    server_name _;
    root /usr/share/nginx/html;
    index index.html index.htm;

    location / {
        try_files $uri $uri/ =404;
    }
}
NGINX

nginx -t
nginx -s reload 2>/dev/null || systemctl reload nginx
```

parsed.description: server-c3 is running an entrypoint script that continuously kills Nginx worker processes. The Nginx master process respawns workers, but the background loop kills them again every 8 seconds, causing repeated worker crashes and intermittent service instability for traffic handled by this server.
parsed.issue: Nginx workers are repeatedly killed, creating an ongoing worker crash/respawn loop and intermittent service instability. Root cause: server-c3 entrypoint.sh starts a background loop that identifies children of the nginx master and kills them every 8 seconds.
parsed.remediation:
Remove the intentional worker-killing loop from `entrypoint.sh`, redeploy the corrected entrypoint, and restart the service/container so Nginx workers are no longer terminated.

Run on `server-c3`:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

ENTRYPOINT="/entrypoint.sh"

if [[ ! -f "$ENTRYPOINT" ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: $ENTRYPOINT not found. Locate the active entrypoint.sh and update ENTRYPOINT accordingly." >&2
  exit 1
fi

BACKUP="${ENTRYPOINT}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
cp -a "$ENTRYPOINT" "$BACKUP"
echo "Backed up $ENTRYPOINT to $BACKUP"

# Remove the known bad background loop that kills Nginx worker children every 8 seconds.
# This targets blocks containing: while/sleep loop + nginx master child discovery + kill.
python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
import re

path = Path('/entrypoint.sh')
text = path.read_text()

patterns = [
    # Common one-line or compact background loop form.
    re.compile(r'''\n?\s*\(?\s*while\s+true\s*;?\s*do\s*.*?(?:pgrep|ps).*?(?:nginx).*?(?:master).*?kill.*?sleep\s+8\s*;?\s*done\s*\)?\s*&\s*\n?''', re.I | re.S),
    # Multi-line loop where kill occurs before sleep 8.
    re.compile(r'''\n?\s*while\s+true\s*;?\s*do\s*\n(?:(?!done).)*?(?:pgrep|ps).*(?:nginx|master).*\n(?:(?!done).)*?kill.*\n(?:(?!done).)*?sleep\s+8\s*\n\s*done\s*&?\s*\n?''', re.I | re.S),
]

new = text
for pat in patterns:
    new = pat.sub('\n# Removed bad loop: do not kill nginx workers from entrypoint.sh\n', new)

if new == text:
    raise SystemExit('ERROR: No matching nginx worker-killing loop was removed. Manually remove the loop that finds nginx master children and kills them every 8 seconds, then rerun validation.')

path.write_text(new)
PY

chmod +x "$ENTRYPOINT"

# Validate that the bad behavior is gone from the entrypoint.
if grep -Eiq 'kill|pkill|pgrep|sleep 8|nginx.*master' "$ENTRYPOINT"; then
  echo "Review $ENTRYPOINT to ensure no remaining loop kills nginx workers:" >&2
  grep -Ein 'kill|pkill|pgrep|sleep 8|nginx.*master' "$ENTRYPOINT" >&2 || true
fi

# Restart the affected service. Use the command appropriate for this host.
if systemctl list-unit-files | grep -q '^nginx\.service'; then
  systemctl restart nginx
  systemctl status nginx --no-pager
elif command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  # Replace nginx_container_name if needed.
  docker ps --format '{{.Names}}' | grep -E '^nginx|nginx' || true
  echo "If Nginx is containerized, rebuild/recreate the container image with the fixed entrypoint and restart it, for example:"
  echo "  docker compose up -d --build nginx"
else
  nginx -t && nginx -s reload
fi

# Confirm workers remain stable for longer than the previous 8-second kill interval.
echo "Validating Nginx worker stability for 30 seconds..."
ps -eo pid,ppid,comm,args | grep '[n]ginx'
sleep 30
ps -eo pid,ppid,comm,args | grep '[n]ginx'
echo "Remediation complete if worker PIDs are not being killed/recreated every 8 seconds and logs show no worker exits caused by SIGTERM/SIGKILL from entrypoint.sh."
```

parsed.description: On cluster C, server-c4 has an nginx /app route that proxies to an upstream defined as 127.0.0.1:3000. That local backend is unavailable, so requests to /app fail with an upstream/proxy error for users hitting this endpoint.
parsed.issue: The /app endpoint is configured to proxy to an unavailable local backend, so users hitting /app receive an upstream failure. Root cause: server-c4 nginx.conf.template defines an nginx upstream pointing at 127.0.0.1:3000 and a /app proxy_pass to that upstream.
parsed.remediation:
Because the nginx root cause is known, correct the /app upstream so it points to a reachable backend, then validate and reload nginx. Replace `APP_BACKEND_HOST` and `APP_BACKEND_PORT` with the actual application backend for /app.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Run on server-c4.
# Set these to the real, reachable /app backend.
APP_BACKEND_HOST="<REPLACE_WITH_BACKEND_HOST>"
APP_BACKEND_PORT="<REPLACE_WITH_BACKEND_PORT>"
NGINX_TEMPLATE="/etc/nginx/nginx.conf.template"

if [[ "$APP_BACKEND_HOST" == "<REPLACE_WITH_BACKEND_HOST>" || "$APP_BACKEND_PORT" == "<REPLACE_WITH_BACKEND_PORT>" ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: Set APP_BACKEND_HOST and APP_BACKEND_PORT before running." >&2
  exit 1
fi

sudo cp "$NGINX_TEMPLATE" "${NGINX_TEMPLATE}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

# Replace the unavailable local upstream target.
sudo sed -i -E "s#127\.0\.0\.1:3000#${APP_BACKEND_HOST}:${APP_BACKEND_PORT}#g" "$NGINX_TEMPLATE"

# If nginx is generated from the template by a deployment/render step, run that step here.
# Otherwise, apply the same replacement to the active nginx config if present.
if [[ -f /etc/nginx/nginx.conf ]]; then
  sudo cp /etc/nginx/nginx.conf "/etc/nginx/nginx.conf.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
  sudo sed -i -E "s#127\.0\.0\.1:3000#${APP_BACKEND_HOST}:${APP_BACKEND_PORT}#g" /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
fi

sudo nginx -t
sudo systemctl reload nginx

curl -fIs http://127.0.0.1/app >/dev/null && echo "/app is reachable through nginx"
```

If /app is supposed to be served by a local process instead, deploy/start that service so it listens on `127.0.0.1:3000`, then run `sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx` and verify with `curl -fI http://127.0.0.1/app`.

parsed.description: The /app route on server-c2 is returning upstream failures because Nginx is generated with a proxy target of 127.0.0.1:3000, but no backend is available at that local address. This makes the /app endpoint unavailable for users routed to this server.
parsed.issue: The /app endpoint is configured to proxy to an unavailable local backend, so users hitting /app receive an upstream failure. Root cause: server-c2 entrypoint.sh generates an nginx upstream pointing at 127.0.0.1:3000 and a /app proxy_pass to that upstream.
parsed.remediation:
Fix the generated Nginx upstream so /app points to the actual reachable backend, then reload Nginx. Example Bash remediation for server-c2, using environment variables for the correct backend host/port:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Set these to the real backend service for /app before running.
: "${APP_BACKEND_HOST:?Set APP_BACKEND_HOST to the reachable /app backend host}"
: "${APP_BACKEND_PORT:?Set APP_BACKEND_PORT to the reachable /app backend port}"

ENTRYPOINT="/entrypoint.sh"

# Back up current entrypoint.
cp "$ENTRYPOINT" "${ENTRYPOINT}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

# Replace the hard-coded unavailable local upstream with the real backend.
sed -i -E "s/127\.0\.0\.1:3000/${APP_BACKEND_HOST}:${APP_BACKEND_PORT}/g" "$ENTRYPOINT"

# Regenerate Nginx config by re-running the entrypoint if that is the deployment pattern,
# or restart the service/container that runs this entrypoint.
# Then validate and reload Nginx.
nginx -t

parsed.description: server-c2 is bypassing Docker-native log collection for Nginx. Its entrypoint removes the standard Docker log symlinks and configures Nginx access/error logs to ordinary files under /var/log/nginx. Operators may miss important request/error events in `docker logs` or the platform log pipeline, and the container filesystem can grow without bound if those files are not rotated.
parsed.issue: Nginx access and error logs are written to ordinary files inside the container instead of Docker stdout/stderr, reducing operator log visibility and risking unbounded disk growth. Root cause: server-c2 entrypoint.sh removes the Docker log symlinks and configures error_log/access_log to /var/log/nginx files.
parsed.remediation:
Update server-c2's Nginx container entrypoint/config so access logs go to stdout and error logs go to stderr. Remove any entrypoint logic that deletes Docker log symlinks.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Run on server-c2 against the affected Nginx container/image workspace.
# Set these paths if they differ in your deployment.
ENTRYPOINT="./entrypoint.sh"
NGINX_CONF="./nginx.conf"
DEFAULT_CONF="./conf.d/default.conf"

# 1) Stop removing Docker log symlinks from the entrypoint.
if [[ -f "$ENTRYPOINT" ]]; then
  cp "$ENTRYPOINT" "${ENTRYPOINT}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
  sed -i -E '\#rm[[:space:]-f]+/var/log/nginx/(access|error)\.log#d' "$ENTRYPOINT"
  sed -i -E '\#unlink[[:space:]]+/var/log/nginx/(access|error)\.log#d' "$ENTRYPOINT"

  # Ensure the symlinks are present when the container starts.
  if ! grep -q 'ln -sf /dev/stdout /var/log/nginx/access.log' "$ENTRYPOINT"; then
    sed -i '/^[[:space:]]*exec /i \
mkdir -p /var/log/nginx\
ln -sf /dev/stdout /var/log/nginx/access.log\
ln -sf /dev/stderr /var/log/nginx/error.log\
' "$ENTRYPOINT"
  fi
fi

# 2) Configure Nginx to emit directly to Docker stdout/stderr.
for conf in "$NGINX_CONF" "$DEFAULT_CONF"; do
  [[ -f "$conf" ]] || continue
  cp "$conf" "${conf}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
  sed -i -E 's#error_log[[:space:]]+[^;]+;#error_log /dev/stderr warn;#g' "$conf"
  sed -i -E 's#access_log[[:space:]]+[^;]+;#access_log /dev/stdout;#g' "$conf"
done

# 3) Validate before rollout. Run inside the rebuilt/running container as appropriate.
nginx -t
nginx -s reload
```

After redeploying/restarting the container, verify:

```bash
docker logs <nginx_container_name_or_id> --tail 100
# Confirm access/error events appear here.

docker exec <nginx_container_name_or_id> sh -c 'ls -l /var/log/nginx/access.log /var/log/nginx/error.log'
# Expected: access.log -> /dev/stdout and error.log -> /dev/stderr, or Nginx config directly references /dev/stdout and /dev/stderr.
```

kwargs.content:
You are a QA person writing tickets for issues affecting a server network.
            You will examine the provided issue and affected servers, and generate a description, severity and remediation.
            DO NOT edit issue, cluster_id, ticket_id or affected_servers.

            Here are the affected servers: ["server-c3"]

            Here is the cluster_id: C

            Here is the issue: Nginx workers are repeatedly killed, creating an ongoing worker crash/respawn loop and intermittent service instability. Root cause: server-c3 entrypoint.sh starts a background loop that identifies children of the nginx master and kills them every 8 seconds.

            The ticket ID is: bfa5df01-be70-4cbf-9def-6fc15e607586

            Severity: 1 = informational, 2 = minor, 3 = moderate, 4 = major, 5 = critical (service down)

            For the remediation field, apply the following rules strictly:

            - If the issue has a single, unambiguous root cause and a known fix (e.g. a version mismatch
              where the correct version is known), provide executable Bash, Nginx or Ansible code.
              If multiple servers require the same fix, prefer Ansible. Code must be clearly readable
              and executable. You may include brief instructional text alongside the code.

            - If the issue is a symptom whose root cause cannot be determined from the information
              provided (e.g. a server that is not responding — which could be caused by a crash,
              resource exhaustion, a bad config, a network fault, or many other things), do NOT
              provide a fix. Instead, describe the investigation steps an engineer should take to
              determine the cause before attempting a remediation. List the specific commands they
              should run and what to look for in the output.

            The output will be displayed in markdown; feel free to use it.

TicketOutput(ticket_id='bfa5df01-be70-4cbf-9def-6fc15e607586', cluster='C', issue='Nginx workers are repeatedly killed, creating an ongoing worker crash/respawn loop and intermittent service instability. Root cause: server-c3 entrypoint.sh starts a background loop that identifies children of the nginx master and kills them every 8 seconds.', description='server-c3 is running an entrypoint script that continuously kills Nginx worker processes. The Nginx master process respawns workers, but the background loop kills them again every 8 seconds, causing repeated worker crashes and intermittent service instability for traffic handled by this server.', affected_servers=['server-c3'], severity=4, remediation='Remove the intentional worker-killing loop from `entrypoint.sh`, redeploy the corrected entrypoint, and restart the service/container so Nginx workers are no longer terminated.

Run on `server-c3`:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

ENTRYPOINT="/entrypoint.sh"

if [[ ! -f "$ENTRYPOINT" ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: $ENTRYPOINT not found. Locate the active entrypoint.sh and update ENTRYPOINT accordingly." >&2
  exit 1
fi

BACKUP="${ENTRYPOINT}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
cp -a "$ENTRYPOINT" "$BACKUP"
echo "Backed up $ENTRYPOINT to $BACKUP"

# Remove the known bad background loop that kills Nginx worker children every 8 seconds.
# This targets blocks containing: while/sleep loop + nginx master child discovery + kill.
python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
import re

path = Path('/entrypoint.sh')
text = path.read_text()

patterns = [
    # Common one-line or compact background loop form.
    re.compile(r'''\n?\s*\(?\s*while\s+true\s*;?\s*do\s*.*?(?:pgrep|ps).*?(?:nginx).*?(?:master).*?kill.*?sleep\s+8\s*;?\s*done\s*\)?\s*&\s*\n?''', re.I | re.S),
    # Multi-line loop where kill occurs before sleep 8.
    re.compile(r'''\n?\s*while\s+true\s*;?\s*do\s*\n(?:(?!done).)*?(?:pgrep|ps).*(?:nginx|master).*\n(?:(?!done).)*?kill.*\n(?:(?!done).)*?sleep\s+8\s*\n\s*done\s*&?\s*\n?''', re.I | re.S),
]

new = text
for pat in patterns:
    new = pat.sub('\n# Removed bad loop: do not kill nginx workers from entrypoint.sh\n', new)

if new == text:
    raise SystemExit('ERROR: No matching nginx worker-killing loop was removed. Manually remove the loop that finds nginx master children and kills them every 8 seconds, then rerun validation.')

path.write_text(new)
PY

chmod +x "$ENTRYPOINT"

# Validate that the bad behavior is gone from the entrypoint.
if grep -Eiq 'kill|pkill|pgrep|sleep 8|nginx.*master' "$ENTRYPOINT"; then
  echo "Review $ENTRYPOINT to ensure no remaining loop kills nginx workers:" >&2
  grep -Ein 'kill|pkill|pgrep|sleep 8|nginx.*master' "$ENTRYPOINT" >&2 || true
fi

# Restart the affected service. Use the command appropriate for this host.
if systemctl list-unit-files | grep -q '^nginx\.service'; then
  systemctl restart nginx
  systemctl status nginx --no-pager
elif command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  # Replace nginx_container_name if needed.
  docker ps --format '{{.Names}}' | grep -E '^nginx|nginx' || true
  echo "If Nginx is containerized, rebuild/recreate the container image with the fixed entrypoint and restart it, for example:"
  echo "  docker compose up -d --build nginx"
else
  nginx -t && nginx -s reload
fi

# Confirm workers remain stable for longer than the previous 8-second kill interval.
echo "Validating Nginx worker stability for 30 seconds..."
ps -eo pid,ppid,comm,args | grep '[n]ginx'
sleep 30
ps -eo pid,ppid,comm,args | grep '[n]ginx'
echo "Remediation complete if worker PIDs are not being killed/recreated every 8 seconds and logs show no worker exits caused by SIGTERM/SIGKILL from entrypoint.sh."
```')

Root cause: server-c1 entrypoint.sh appends an unterminated server block containing an invalid directive to /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf after nginx has started.', description='server-c1 is currently serving traffic from the configuration loaded at startup, but /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf has been corrupted afterward by the entrypoint script. As a result, nginx configuration validation and reloads fail, preventing safe configuration changes or service reloads and creating operational risk on the next restart/redeploy.', affected_servers=['server-c1'], severity=4, remediation='Run this on server-c1 as root. It backs up the files, removes entrypoint lines/heredocs that append to /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf, restores a valid default server config, validates it, and reloads Nginx. If server-c1 is container/image-built, also apply this entrypoint change in the image/source repo and redeploy so it persists.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

CONF=/etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
ENTRYPOINT=${ENTRYPOINT:-}
if [[ -z "$ENTRYPOINT" ]]; then
  for p in /entrypoint.sh /usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh /docker-entrypoint.sh /usr/local/bin/docker-entrypoint.sh; do
    [[ -f "$p" ]] && ENTRYPOINT="$p" && break
  done
fi
[[ -f "$ENTRYPOINT" ]] || { echo "Set ENTRYPOINT=/path/to/entrypoint.sh and rerun" >&2; exit 1; }

ts=$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)
cp -a "$ENTRYPOINT" "$ENTRYPOINT.bak.$ts"
cp -a "$CONF" "$CONF.bak.$ts"

tmp=$(mktemp)
skip=0
delim=
while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
  if [[ "$skip" -eq 1 ]]; then
    trimmed=$(printf '%s\n' "$line" | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//;s/[[:space:]]*$//')
    [[ "$trimmed" == "$delim" ]] && skip=0
    continue
  fi

  if [[ "$line" == *"$CONF"* && ( "$line" == *">>"* || "$line" =~ tee[[:space:]]+-a ) ]]; then
    if [[ "$line" == *"<<"* ]]; then
      delim=${line#*<<}
      delim=${delim#-}
      delim=$(printf '%s\n' "$delim" | awk '{print $1}' | tr -d "'\"")
      skip=1
    fi
    continue
  fi

  printf '%s\n' "$line" >> "$tmp"
done < "$ENTRYPOINT"
cat "$tmp" > "$ENTRYPOINT"
rm -f "$tmp"
chmod +x "$ENTRYPOINT"

cat > "$CONF" <<'NGINX'
server {
    listen 80 default_server;
    listen [::]:80 default_server;
    server_name _;
    root /usr/share/nginx/html;
    index index.html index.htm;

    location / {
        try_files $uri $uri/ =404;
    }
}
NGINX

nginx -t
nginx -s reload 2>/dev/null || systemctl reload nginx
```')

            Here is the cluster_id: C

            Here is the issue: Nginx workers are repeatedly killed, creating an ongoing worker crash/respawn loop and intermittent service instability. Root cause: server-c3 entrypoint.sh starts a background loop that identifies children of the nginx master and kills them every 8 seconds.

            The ticket ID is: bfa5df01-be70-4cbf-9def-6fc15e607586

            Severity: 1 = informational, 2 = minor, 3 = moderate, 4 = major, 5 = critical (service down)

            For the remediation field, apply the following rules strictly:

            - If the issue is a symptom whose root cause cannot be determined from the information

output.description: server-c3 is running an entrypoint script that continuously kills Nginx worker processes. The Nginx master process respawns workers, but the background loop kills them again every 8 seconds, causing repeated worker crashes and intermittent service instability for traffic handled by this server.
output.issue: Nginx workers are repeatedly killed, creating an ongoing worker crash/respawn loop and intermittent service instability. Root cause: server-c3 entrypoint.sh starts a background loop that identifies children of the nginx master and kills them every 8 seconds.
output.remediation:
Remove the intentional worker-killing loop from `entrypoint.sh`, redeploy the corrected entrypoint, and restart the service/container so Nginx workers are no longer terminated.

Run on `server-c3`:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

ENTRYPOINT="/entrypoint.sh"

if [[ ! -f "$ENTRYPOINT" ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: $ENTRYPOINT not found. Locate the active entrypoint.sh and update ENTRYPOINT accordingly." >&2
  exit 1
fi

BACKUP="${ENTRYPOINT}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
cp -a "$ENTRYPOINT" "$BACKUP"
echo "Backed up $ENTRYPOINT to $BACKUP"

# Remove the known bad background loop that kills Nginx worker children every 8 seconds.
# This targets blocks containing: while/sleep loop + nginx master child discovery + kill.
python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
import re

path = Path('/entrypoint.sh')
text = path.read_text()

patterns = [
    # Common one-line or compact background loop form.
    re.compile(r'''\n?\s*\(?\s*while\s+true\s*;?\s*do\s*.*?(?:pgrep|ps).*?(?:nginx).*?(?:master).*?kill.*?sleep\s+8\s*;?\s*done\s*\)?\s*&\s*\n?''', re.I | re.S),
    # Multi-line loop where kill occurs before sleep 8.
    re.compile(r'''\n?\s*while\s+true\s*;?\s*do\s*\n(?:(?!done).)*?(?:pgrep|ps).*(?:nginx|master).*\n(?:(?!done).)*?kill.*\n(?:(?!done).)*?sleep\s+8\s*\n\s*done\s*&?\s*\n?''', re.I | re.S),
]

new = text
for pat in patterns:
    new = pat.sub('\n# Removed bad loop: do not kill nginx workers from entrypoint.sh\n', new)

if new == text:
    raise SystemExit('ERROR: No matching nginx worker-killing loop was removed. Manually remove the loop that finds nginx master children and kills them every 8 seconds, then rerun validation.')

path.write_text(new)
PY

chmod +x "$ENTRYPOINT"

# Validate that the bad behavior is gone from the entrypoint.
if grep -Eiq 'kill|pkill|pgrep|sleep 8|nginx.*master' "$ENTRYPOINT"; then
  echo "Review $ENTRYPOINT to ensure no remaining loop kills nginx workers:" >&2
  grep -Ein 'kill|pkill|pgrep|sleep 8|nginx.*master' "$ENTRYPOINT" >&2 || true
fi

# Restart the affected service. Use the command appropriate for this host.
if systemctl list-unit-files | grep -q '^nginx\.service'; then
  systemctl restart nginx
  systemctl status nginx --no-pager
elif command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  # Replace nginx_container_name if needed.
  docker ps --format '{{.Names}}' | grep -E '^nginx|nginx' || true
  echo "If Nginx is containerized, rebuild/recreate the container image with the fixed entrypoint and restart it, for example:"
  echo "  docker compose up -d --build nginx"
else
  nginx -t && nginx -s reload
fi

# Confirm workers remain stable for longer than the previous 8-second kill interval.
echo "Validating Nginx worker stability for 30 seconds..."
ps -eo pid,ppid,comm,args | grep '[n]ginx'
sleep 30
ps -eo pid,ppid,comm,args | grep '[n]ginx'
echo "Remediation complete if worker PIDs are not being killed/recreated every 8 seconds and logs show no worker exits caused by SIGTERM/SIGKILL from entrypoint.sh."
```

TicketOutput(ticket_id='c58e966b-fd2b-4045-b648-fddaf53070d2', cluster='C', issue='Nginx access and error logs are written to ordinary files inside the container instead of Docker stdout/stderr, reducing operator log visibility and risking unbounded disk growth. Root cause: server-c2 entrypoint.sh removes the Docker log symlinks and configures error_log/access_log to /var/log/nginx files.', description='server-c2 is bypassing Docker-native log collection for Nginx. Its entrypoint removes the standard Docker log symlinks and configures Nginx access/error logs to ordinary files under /var/log/nginx. Operators may miss important request/error events in `docker logs` or the platform log pipeline, and the container filesystem can grow without bound if those files are not rotated.', affected_servers=['server-c2'], severity=3, remediation='Update server-c2's Nginx container entrypoint/config so access logs go to stdout and error logs go to stderr. Remove any entrypoint logic that deletes Docker log symlinks.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Run on server-c2 against the affected Nginx container/image workspace.
# Set these paths if they differ in your deployment.
ENTRYPOINT="./entrypoint.sh"
NGINX_CONF="./nginx.conf"
DEFAULT_CONF="./conf.d/default.conf"

# 1) Stop removing Docker log symlinks from the entrypoint.
if [[ -f "$ENTRYPOINT" ]]; then
  cp "$ENTRYPOINT" "${ENTRYPOINT}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
  sed -i -E '\#rm[[:space:]-f]+/var/log/nginx/(access|error)\.log#d' "$ENTRYPOINT"
  sed -i -E '\#unlink[[:space:]]+/var/log/nginx/(access|error)\.log#d' "$ENTRYPOINT"

  # Ensure the symlinks are present when the container starts.
  if ! grep -q 'ln -sf /dev/stdout /var/log/nginx/access.log' "$ENTRYPOINT"; then
    sed -i '/^[[:space:]]*exec /i \
mkdir -p /var/log/nginx\
ln -sf /dev/stdout /var/log/nginx/access.log\
ln -sf /dev/stderr /var/log/nginx/error.log\
' "$ENTRYPOINT"
  fi
fi

# 2) Configure Nginx to emit directly to Docker stdout/stderr.
for conf in "$NGINX_CONF" "$DEFAULT_CONF"; do
  [[ -f "$conf" ]] || continue
  cp "$conf" "${conf}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
  sed -i -E 's#error_log[[:space:]]+[^;]+;#error_log /dev/stderr warn;#g' "$conf"
  sed -i -E 's#access_log[[:space:]]+[^;]+;#access_log /dev/stdout;#g' "$conf"
done

# 3) Validate before rollout. Run inside the rebuilt/running container as appropriate.
nginx -t
nginx -s reload
```

# Expected: access.log -> /dev/stdout and error.log -> /dev/stderr, or Nginx config directly references /dev/stdout and /dev/stderr.
```')

  cp "$ENTRYPOINT" "${ENTRYPOINT}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
  sed -i -E '\#rm[[:space:]-f]+/var/log/nginx/(access|error)\.log#d' "$ENTRYPOINT"
  sed -i -E '\#unlink[[:space:]]+/var/log/nginx/(access|error)\.log#d' "$ENTRYPOINT"

  # Ensure the symlinks are present when the container starts.
  if ! grep -q 'ln -sf /dev/stdout /var/log/nginx/access.log' "$ENTRYPOINT"; then
    sed -i '/^[[:space:]]*exec /i \
mkdir -p /var/log/nginx\
ln -sf /dev/stdout /var/log/nginx/access.log\
ln -sf /dev/stderr /var/log/nginx/error.log\
' "$ENTRYPOINT"
  fi
fi

# 2) Configure Nginx to emit directly to Docker stdout/stderr.
for conf in "$NGINX_CONF" "$DEFAULT_CONF"; do
  [[ -f "$conf" ]] || continue
  cp "$conf" "${conf}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
  sed -i -E 's#error_log[[:space:]]+[^;]+;#error_log /dev/stderr warn;#g' "$conf"
  sed -i -E 's#access_log[[:space:]]+[^;]+;#access_log /dev/stdout;#g' "$conf"
done

# 3) Validate before rollout. Run inside the rebuilt/running container as appropriate.
nginx -t
nginx -s reload
```

After redeploying/restarting the container, verify:

```bash
docker logs <nginx_container_name_or_id> --tail 100
# Confirm access/error events appear here.

# Expected: access.log -> /dev/stdout and error.log -> /dev/stderr, or Nginx config directly references /dev/stdout and /dev/stderr.

You are a QA person writing tickets for issues affecting a server network.
            You will examine the provided issue and affected servers, and generate a description, severity and remediation.
            DO NOT edit issue, cluster_id, ticket_id or affected_servers.

            Here are the affected servers: ["server-c4"]

            Here is the cluster_id: C

            Here is the issue: The /app endpoint is configured to proxy to an unavailable local backend, so users hitting /app receive an upstream failure. Root cause: server-c4 nginx.conf.template defines an nginx upstream pointing at 127.0.0.1:3000 and a /app proxy_pass to that upstream.

            The ticket ID is: 8dea3223-c4e0-47be-ba8d-bdfcfd32ed51

            Severity: 1 = informational, 2 = minor, 3 = moderate, 4 = major, 5 = critical (service down)

            For the remediation field, apply the following rules strictly:

            - If the issue has a single, unambiguous root cause and a known fix (e.g. a version mismatch
              where the correct version is known), provide executable Bash, Nginx or Ansible code.
              If multiple servers require the same fix, prefer Ansible. Code must be clearly readable
              and executable. You may include brief instructional text alongside the code.

            - If the issue is a symptom whose root cause cannot be determined from the information
              provided (e.g. a server that is not responding — which could be caused by a crash,
              resource exhaustion, a bad config, a network fault, or many other things), do NOT
              provide a fix. Instead, describe the investigation steps an engineer should take to
              determine the cause before attempting a remediation. List the specific commands they
              should run and what to look for in the output.

            The output will be displayed in markdown; feel free to use it.

output.description: On cluster C, server-c4 has an nginx /app route that proxies to an upstream defined as 127.0.0.1:3000. That local backend is unavailable, so requests to /app fail with an upstream/proxy error for users hitting this endpoint.
output.issue: The /app endpoint is configured to proxy to an unavailable local backend, so users hitting /app receive an upstream failure. Root cause: server-c4 nginx.conf.template defines an nginx upstream pointing at 127.0.0.1:3000 and a /app proxy_pass to that upstream.
output.remediation:
Because the nginx root cause is known, correct the /app upstream so it points to a reachable backend, then validate and reload nginx. Replace `APP_BACKEND_HOST` and `APP_BACKEND_PORT` with the actual application backend for /app.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Run on server-c4.
# Set these to the real, reachable /app backend.
APP_BACKEND_HOST="<REPLACE_WITH_BACKEND_HOST>"
APP_BACKEND_PORT="<REPLACE_WITH_BACKEND_PORT>"
NGINX_TEMPLATE="/etc/nginx/nginx.conf.template"

if [[ "$APP_BACKEND_HOST" == "<REPLACE_WITH_BACKEND_HOST>" || "$APP_BACKEND_PORT" == "<REPLACE_WITH_BACKEND_PORT>" ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: Set APP_BACKEND_HOST and APP_BACKEND_PORT before running." >&2
  exit 1
fi

sudo cp "$NGINX_TEMPLATE" "${NGINX_TEMPLATE}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

# Replace the unavailable local upstream target.
sudo sed -i -E "s#127\.0\.0\.1:3000#${APP_BACKEND_HOST}:${APP_BACKEND_PORT}#g" "$NGINX_TEMPLATE"

# If nginx is generated from the template by a deployment/render step, run that step here.
# Otherwise, apply the same replacement to the active nginx config if present.
if [[ -f /etc/nginx/nginx.conf ]]; then
  sudo cp /etc/nginx/nginx.conf "/etc/nginx/nginx.conf.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
  sudo sed -i -E "s#127\.0\.0\.1:3000#${APP_BACKEND_HOST}:${APP_BACKEND_PORT}#g" /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
fi

sudo nginx -t
sudo systemctl reload nginx

curl -fIs http://127.0.0.1/app >/dev/null && echo "/app is reachable through nginx"
```

If /app is supposed to be served by a local process instead, deploy/start that service so it listens on `127.0.0.1:3000`, then run `sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx` and verify with `curl -fI http://127.0.0.1/app`.

            Here are the affected servers: ["server-c3"]

            Here is the cluster_id: C

            Here is the issue: Nginx workers are repeatedly killed, creating an ongoing worker crash/respawn loop and intermittent service instability. Root cause: server-c3 entrypoint.sh starts a background loop that identifies children of the nginx master and kills them every 8 seconds.

            The ticket ID is: bfa5df01-be70-4cbf-9def-6fc15e607586

            Severity: 1 = informational, 2 = minor, 3 = moderate, 4 = major, 5 = critical (service down)

            For the remediation field, apply the following rules strictly:

            - If the issue has a single, unambiguous root cause and a known fix (e.g. a version mismatch
              where the correct version is known), provide executable Bash, Nginx or Ansible code.
              If multiple servers require the same fix, prefer Ansible. Code must be clearly readable
              and executable. You may include brief instructional text alongside the code.

            - If the issue is a symptom whose root cause cannot be determined from the information
              provided (e.g. a server that is not responding — which could be caused by a crash,
              resource exhaustion, a bad config, a network fault, or many other things), do NOT
              provide a fix. Instead, describe the investigation steps an engineer should take to
              determine the cause before attempting a remediation. List the specific commands they
              should run and what to look for in the output.

            The output will be displayed in markdown; feel free to use it.

output.description: server-c3 is running an entrypoint script that continuously kills Nginx worker processes. The Nginx master process respawns workers, but the background loop kills them again every 8 seconds, causing repeated worker crashes and intermittent service instability for traffic handled by this server.
output.issue: Nginx workers are repeatedly killed, creating an ongoing worker crash/respawn loop and intermittent service instability. Root cause: server-c3 entrypoint.sh starts a background loop that identifies children of the nginx master and kills them every 8 seconds.
output.remediation:
Remove the intentional worker-killing loop from `entrypoint.sh`, redeploy the corrected entrypoint, and restart the service/container so Nginx workers are no longer terminated.

Run on `server-c3`:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

ENTRYPOINT="/entrypoint.sh"

if [[ ! -f "$ENTRYPOINT" ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: $ENTRYPOINT not found. Locate the active entrypoint.sh and update ENTRYPOINT accordingly." >&2
  exit 1
fi

BACKUP="${ENTRYPOINT}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
cp -a "$ENTRYPOINT" "$BACKUP"
echo "Backed up $ENTRYPOINT to $BACKUP"

# Remove the known bad background loop that kills Nginx worker children every 8 seconds.
# This targets blocks containing: while/sleep loop + nginx master child discovery + kill.
python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
import re

path = Path('/entrypoint.sh')
text = path.read_text()

patterns = [
    # Common one-line or compact background loop form.
    re.compile(r'''\n?\s*\(?\s*while\s+true\s*;?\s*do\s*.*?(?:pgrep|ps).*?(?:nginx).*?(?:master).*?kill.*?sleep\s+8\s*;?\s*done\s*\)?\s*&\s*\n?''', re.I | re.S),
    # Multi-line loop where kill occurs before sleep 8.
    re.compile(r'''\n?\s*while\s+true\s*;?\s*do\s*\n(?:(?!done).)*?(?:pgrep|ps).*(?:nginx|master).*\n(?:(?!done).)*?kill.*\n(?:(?!done).)*?sleep\s+8\s*\n\s*done\s*&?\s*\n?''', re.I | re.S),
]

new = text
for pat in patterns:
    new = pat.sub('\n# Removed bad loop: do not kill nginx workers from entrypoint.sh\n', new)

if new == text:
    raise SystemExit('ERROR: No matching nginx worker-killing loop was removed. Manually remove the loop that finds nginx master children and kills them every 8 seconds, then rerun validation.')

path.write_text(new)
PY

chmod +x "$ENTRYPOINT"

# Validate that the bad behavior is gone from the entrypoint.
if grep -Eiq 'kill|pkill|pgrep|sleep 8|nginx.*master' "$ENTRYPOINT"; then
  echo "Review $ENTRYPOINT to ensure no remaining loop kills nginx workers:" >&2
  grep -Ein 'kill|pkill|pgrep|sleep 8|nginx.*master' "$ENTRYPOINT" >&2 || true
fi

# Restart the affected service. Use the command appropriate for this host.
if systemctl list-unit-files | grep -q '^nginx\.service'; then
  systemctl restart nginx
  systemctl status nginx --no-pager
elif command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  # Replace nginx_container_name if needed.
  docker ps --format '{{.Names}}' | grep -E '^nginx|nginx' || true
  echo "If Nginx is containerized, rebuild/recreate the container image with the fixed entrypoint and restart it, for example:"
  echo "  docker compose up -d --build nginx"
else
  nginx -t && nginx -s reload
fi

# Confirm workers remain stable for longer than the previous 8-second kill interval.
echo "Validating Nginx worker stability for 30 seconds..."
ps -eo pid,ppid,comm,args | grep '[n]ginx'
sleep 30
ps -eo pid,ppid,comm,args | grep '[n]ginx'
echo "Remediation complete if worker PIDs are not being killed/recreated every 8 seconds and logs show no worker exits caused by SIGTERM/SIGKILL from entrypoint.sh."
```

As a result, nginx configuration validation and reloads fail, preventing safe configuration changes or service reloads and creating operational risk on the next restart/redeploy.
output.issue: Nginx continues serving but the active configuration file is deliberately corrupted after startup, making config validation/reload fail. Root cause: server-c1 entrypoint.sh appends an unterminated server block containing an invalid directive to /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf after nginx has started.
output.remediation:
Run this on server-c1 as root. It backs up the files, removes entrypoint lines/heredocs that append to /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf, restores a valid default server config, validates it, and reloads Nginx. If server-c1 is container/image-built, also apply this entrypoint change in the image/source repo and redeploy so it persists.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

CONF=/etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
ENTRYPOINT=${ENTRYPOINT:-}
if [[ -z "$ENTRYPOINT" ]]; then
  for p in /entrypoint.sh /usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh /docker-entrypoint.sh /usr/local/bin/docker-entrypoint.sh; do
    [[ -f "$p" ]] && ENTRYPOINT="$p" && break
  done
fi
[[ -f "$ENTRYPOINT" ]] || { echo "Set ENTRYPOINT=/path/to/entrypoint.sh and rerun" >&2; exit 1; }

ts=$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)
cp -a "$ENTRYPOINT" "$ENTRYPOINT.bak.$ts"
cp -a "$CONF" "$CONF.bak.$ts"

tmp=$(mktemp)
skip=0
delim=
while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
  if [[ "$skip" -eq 1 ]]; then
    trimmed=$(printf '%s\n' "$line" | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//;s/[[:space:]]*$//')
    [[ "$trimmed" == "$delim" ]] && skip=0
    continue
  fi

  if [[ "$line" == *"$CONF"* && ( "$line" == *">>"* || "$line" =~ tee[[:space:]]+-a ) ]]; then
    if [[ "$line" == *"<<"* ]]; then
      delim=${line#*<<}
      delim=${delim#-}
      delim=$(printf '%s\n' "$delim" | awk '{print $1}' | tr -d "'\"")
      skip=1
    fi
    continue
  fi

  printf '%s\n' "$line" >> "$tmp"
done < "$ENTRYPOINT"
cat "$tmp" > "$ENTRYPOINT"
rm -f "$tmp"
chmod +x "$ENTRYPOINT"

cat > "$CONF" <<'NGINX'
server {
    listen 80 default_server;
    listen [::]:80 default_server;
    server_name _;
    root /usr/share/nginx/html;
    index index.html index.htm;

    location / {
        try_files $uri $uri/ =404;
    }
}

nginx -t
nginx -s reload 2>/dev/null || systemctl reload nginx
```

output.description: On cluster C, server-c4 has an nginx /app route that proxies to an upstream defined as 127.0.0.1:3000. That local backend is unavailable, so requests to /app fail with an upstream/proxy error for users hitting this endpoint.
output.issue: The /app endpoint is configured to proxy to an unavailable local backend, so users hitting /app receive an upstream failure. Root cause: server-c4 nginx.conf.template defines an nginx upstream pointing at 127.0.0.1:3000 and a /app proxy_pass to that upstream.
output.remediation:
Because the nginx root cause is known, correct the /app upstream so it points to a reachable backend, then validate and reload nginx. Replace `APP_BACKEND_HOST` and `APP_BACKEND_PORT` with the actual application backend for /app.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Run on server-c4.
# Set these to the real, reachable /app backend.
APP_BACKEND_HOST="<REPLACE_WITH_BACKEND_HOST>"
APP_BACKEND_PORT="<REPLACE_WITH_BACKEND_PORT>"
NGINX_TEMPLATE="/etc/nginx/nginx.conf.template"

if [[ "$APP_BACKEND_HOST" == "<REPLACE_WITH_BACKEND_HOST>" || "$APP_BACKEND_PORT" == "<REPLACE_WITH_BACKEND_PORT>" ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: Set APP_BACKEND_HOST and APP_BACKEND_PORT before running." >&2
  exit 1
fi

sudo cp "$NGINX_TEMPLATE" "${NGINX_TEMPLATE}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

# Replace the unavailable local upstream target.
sudo sed -i -E "s#127\.0\.0\.1:3000#${APP_BACKEND_HOST}:${APP_BACKEND_PORT}#g" "$NGINX_TEMPLATE"

# If nginx is generated from the template by a deployment/render step, run that step here.
# Otherwise, apply the same replacement to the active nginx config if present.
if [[ -f /etc/nginx/nginx.conf ]]; then
  sudo cp /etc/nginx/nginx.conf "/etc/nginx/nginx.conf.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
  sudo sed -i -E "s#127\.0\.0\.1:3000#${APP_BACKEND_HOST}:${APP_BACKEND_PORT}#g" /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
fi

sudo nginx -t
sudo systemctl reload nginx

curl -fIs http://127.0.0.1/app >/dev/null && echo "/app is reachable through nginx"
```

If /app is supposed to be served by a local process instead, deploy/start that service so it listens on `127.0.0.1:3000`, then run `sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx` and verify with `curl -fI http://127.0.0.1/app`.

output.description: server-c2 is bypassing Docker-native log collection for Nginx. Its entrypoint removes the standard Docker log symlinks and configures Nginx access/error logs to ordinary files under /var/log/nginx. Operators may miss important request/error events in `docker logs` or the platform log pipeline, and the container filesystem can grow without bound if those files are not rotated.
output.issue: Nginx access and error logs are written to ordinary files inside the container instead of Docker stdout/stderr, reducing operator log visibility and risking unbounded disk growth. Root cause: server-c2 entrypoint.sh removes the Docker log symlinks and configures error_log/access_log to /var/log/nginx files.
output.remediation:
Update server-c2's Nginx container entrypoint/config so access logs go to stdout and error logs go to stderr. Remove any entrypoint logic that deletes Docker log symlinks.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Run on server-c2 against the affected Nginx container/image workspace.
# Set these paths if they differ in your deployment.
ENTRYPOINT="./entrypoint.sh"
NGINX_CONF="./nginx.conf"
DEFAULT_CONF="./conf.d/default.conf"

# 1) Stop removing Docker log symlinks from the entrypoint.
if [[ -f "$ENTRYPOINT" ]]; then
  cp "$ENTRYPOINT" "${ENTRYPOINT}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
  sed -i -E '\#rm[[:space:]-f]+/var/log/nginx/(access|error)\.log#d' "$ENTRYPOINT"
  sed -i -E '\#unlink[[:space:]]+/var/log/nginx/(access|error)\.log#d' "$ENTRYPOINT"

  # Ensure the symlinks are present when the container starts.
  if ! grep -q 'ln -sf /dev/stdout /var/log/nginx/access.log' "$ENTRYPOINT"; then
    sed -i '/^[[:space:]]*exec /i \
mkdir -p /var/log/nginx\
ln -sf /dev/stdout /var/log/nginx/access.log\
ln -sf /dev/stderr /var/log/nginx/error.log\
' "$ENTRYPOINT"
  fi
fi

# 2) Configure Nginx to emit directly to Docker stdout/stderr.
for conf in "$NGINX_CONF" "$DEFAULT_CONF"; do
  [[ -f "$conf" ]] || continue
  cp "$conf" "${conf}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
  sed -i -E 's#error_log[[:space:]]+[^;]+;#error_log /dev/stderr warn;#g' "$conf"
  sed -i -E 's#access_log[[:space:]]+[^;]+;#access_log /dev/stdout;#g' "$conf"
done

# 3) Validate before rollout. Run inside the rebuilt/running container as appropriate.
nginx -t
nginx -s reload
```

After redeploying/restarting the container, verify:

```bash
docker logs <nginx_container_name_or_id> --tail 100
# Confirm access/error events appear here.

docker exec <nginx_container_name_or_id> sh -c 'ls -l /var/log/nginx/access.log /var/log/nginx/error.log'
# Expected: access.log -> /dev/stdout and error.log -> /dev/stderr, or Nginx config directly references /dev/stdout and /dev/stderr.
```

inputs.issue: Nginx workers are repeatedly killed, creating an ongoing worker crash/respawn loop and intermittent service instability. Root cause: server-c3 entrypoint.sh starts a background loop that identifies children of the nginx master and kills them every 8 seconds.

tickets_created.description: server-c3 is running an entrypoint script that continuously kills Nginx worker processes. The Nginx master process respawns workers, but the background loop kills them again every 8 seconds, causing repeated worker crashes and intermittent service instability for traffic handled by this server.
tickets_created.issue: Nginx workers are repeatedly killed, creating an ongoing worker crash/respawn loop and intermittent service instability. Root cause: server-c3 entrypoint.sh starts a background loop that identifies children of the nginx master and kills them every 8 seconds.
tickets_created.remediation:
Remove the intentional worker-killing loop from `entrypoint.sh`, redeploy the corrected entrypoint, and restart the service/container so Nginx workers are no longer terminated.

Run on `server-c3`:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

ENTRYPOINT="/entrypoint.sh"

if [[ ! -f "$ENTRYPOINT" ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: $ENTRYPOINT not found. Locate the active entrypoint.sh and update ENTRYPOINT accordingly." >&2
  exit 1
fi

BACKUP="${ENTRYPOINT}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
cp -a "$ENTRYPOINT" "$BACKUP"
echo "Backed up $ENTRYPOINT to $BACKUP"

# Remove the known bad background loop that kills Nginx worker children every 8 seconds.
# This targets blocks containing: while/sleep loop + nginx master child discovery + kill.
python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
import re

path = Path('/entrypoint.sh')
text = path.read_text()

patterns = [
    # Common one-line or compact background loop form.
    re.compile(r'''\n?\s*\(?\s*while\s+true\s*;?\s*do\s*.*?(?:pgrep|ps).*?(?:nginx).*?(?:master).*?kill.*?sleep\s+8\s*;?\s*done\s*\)?\s*&\s*\n?''', re.I | re.S),
    # Multi-line loop where kill occurs before sleep 8.
    re.compile(r'''\n?\s*while\s+true\s*;?\s*do\s*\n(?:(?!done).)*?(?:pgrep|ps).*(?:nginx|master).*\n(?:(?!done).)*?kill.*\n(?:(?!done).)*?sleep\s+8\s*\n\s*done\s*&?\s*\n?''', re.I | re.S),
]

new = text
for pat in patterns:
    new = pat.sub('\n# Removed bad loop: do not kill nginx workers from entrypoint.sh\n', new)

if new == text:
    raise SystemExit('ERROR: No matching nginx worker-killing loop was removed. Manually remove the loop that finds nginx master children and kills them every 8 seconds, then rerun validation.')

path.write_text(new)
PY

chmod +x "$ENTRYPOINT"

# Validate that the bad behavior is gone from the entrypoint.
if grep -Eiq 'kill|pkill|pgrep|sleep 8|nginx.*master' "$ENTRYPOINT"; then
  echo "Review $ENTRYPOINT to ensure no remaining loop kills nginx workers:" >&2
  grep -Ein 'kill|pkill|pgrep|sleep 8|nginx.*master' "$ENTRYPOINT" >&2 || true
fi

# Restart the affected service. Use the command appropriate for this host.
if systemctl list-unit-files | grep -q '^nginx\.service'; then
  systemctl restart nginx
  systemctl status nginx --no-pager
elif command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  # Replace nginx_container_name if needed.
  docker ps --format '{{.Names}}' | grep -E '^nginx|nginx' || true
  echo "If Nginx is containerized, rebuild/recreate the container image with the fixed entrypoint and restart it, for example:"
  echo "  docker compose up -d --build nginx"
else
  nginx -t && nginx -s reload
fi

# Confirm workers remain stable for longer than the previous 8-second kill interval.
echo "Validating Nginx worker stability for 30 seconds..."
ps -eo pid,ppid,comm,args | grep '[n]ginx'
sleep 30
ps -eo pid,ppid,comm,args | grep '[n]ginx'
echo "Remediation complete if worker PIDs are not being killed/recreated every 8 seconds and logs show no worker exits caused by SIGTERM/SIGKILL from entrypoint.sh."
```

inputs.issue: Nginx access and error logs are written to ordinary files inside the container instead of Docker stdout/stderr, reducing operator log visibility and risking unbounded disk growth. Root cause: server-c2 entrypoint.sh removes the Docker log symlinks and configures error_log/access_log to /var/log/nginx files.

tickets_created.description: server-c2 is bypassing Docker-native log collection for Nginx. Its entrypoint removes the standard Docker log symlinks and configures Nginx access/error logs to ordinary files under /var/log/nginx. Operators may miss important request/error events in `docker logs` or the platform log pipeline, and the container filesystem can grow without bound if those files are not rotated.
tickets_created.issue: Nginx access and error logs are written to ordinary files inside the container instead of Docker stdout/stderr, reducing operator log visibility and risking unbounded disk growth. Root cause: server-c2 entrypoint.sh removes the Docker log symlinks and configures error_log/access_log to /var/log/nginx files.
tickets_created.remediation:
Update server-c2's Nginx container entrypoint/config so access logs go to stdout and error logs go to stderr. Remove any entrypoint logic that deletes Docker log symlinks.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Run on server-c2 against the affected Nginx container/image workspace.
# Set these paths if they differ in your deployment.
ENTRYPOINT="./entrypoint.sh"
NGINX_CONF="./nginx.conf"
DEFAULT_CONF="./conf.d/default.conf"

# 1) Stop removing Docker log symlinks from the entrypoint.
if [[ -f "$ENTRYPOINT" ]]; then
  cp "$ENTRYPOINT" "${ENTRYPOINT}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
  sed -i -E '\#rm[[:space:]-f]+/var/log/nginx/(access|error)\.log#d' "$ENTRYPOINT"
  sed -i -E '\#unlink[[:space:]]+/var/log/nginx/(access|error)\.log#d' "$ENTRYPOINT"

  # Ensure the symlinks are present when the container starts.
  if ! grep -q 'ln -sf /dev/stdout /var/log/nginx/access.log' "$ENTRYPOINT"; then
    sed -i '/^[[:space:]]*exec /i \
mkdir -p /var/log/nginx\
ln -sf /dev/stdout /var/log/nginx/access.log\
ln -sf /dev/stderr /var/log/nginx/error.log\
' "$ENTRYPOINT"
  fi
fi

# 2) Configure Nginx to emit directly to Docker stdout/stderr.
for conf in "$NGINX_CONF" "$DEFAULT_CONF"; do
  [[ -f "$conf" ]] || continue
  cp "$conf" "${conf}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
  sed -i -E 's#error_log[[:space:]]+[^;]+;#error_log /dev/stderr warn;#g' "$conf"
  sed -i -E 's#access_log[[:space:]]+[^;]+;#access_log /dev/stdout;#g' "$conf"
done

# 3) Validate before rollout. Run inside the rebuilt/running container as appropriate.
nginx -t
nginx -s reload
```

After redeploying/restarting the container, verify:

```bash
docker logs <nginx_container_name_or_id> --tail 100
# Confirm access/error events appear here.

docker exec <nginx_container_name_or_id> sh -c 'ls -l /var/log/nginx/access.log /var/log/nginx/error.log'
# Expected: access.log -> /dev/stdout and error.log -> /dev/stderr, or Nginx config directly references /dev/stdout and /dev/stderr.
```

inputs.issue: The /app endpoint is configured to proxy to an unavailable local backend, so users hitting /app receive an upstream failure. Root cause: server-c2 entrypoint.sh generates an nginx upstream pointing at 127.0.0.1:3000 and a /app proxy_pass to that upstream.

tickets_created.description: The /app route on server-c2 is returning upstream failures because Nginx is generated with a proxy target of 127.0.0.1:3000, but no backend is available at that local address. This makes the /app endpoint unavailable for users routed to this server.
tickets_created.issue: The /app endpoint is configured to proxy to an unavailable local backend, so users hitting /app receive an upstream failure. Root cause: server-c2 entrypoint.sh generates an nginx upstream pointing at 127.0.0.1:3000 and a /app proxy_pass to that upstream.
tickets_created.remediation:
Fix the generated Nginx upstream so /app points to the actual reachable backend, then reload Nginx. Example Bash remediation for server-c2, using environment variables for the correct backend host/port:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Set these to the real backend service for /app before running.
: "${APP_BACKEND_HOST:?Set APP_BACKEND_HOST to the reachable /app backend host}"
: "${APP_BACKEND_PORT:?Set APP_BACKEND_PORT to the reachable /app backend port}"

ENTRYPOINT="/entrypoint.sh"

# Back up current entrypoint.
cp "$ENTRYPOINT" "${ENTRYPOINT}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

# Replace the hard-coded unavailable local upstream with the real backend.
sed -i -E "s/127\.0\.0\.1:3000/${APP_BACKEND_HOST}:${APP_BACKEND_PORT}/g" "$ENTRYPOINT"

# Regenerate Nginx config by re-running the entrypoint if that is the deployment pattern,
# or restart the service/container that runs this entrypoint.
# Then validate and reload Nginx.
nginx -t
nginx -s reload

# Verify the endpoint no longer returns an upstream failure.
curl -fsS -I http://127.0.0.1/app
```

If /app is intended to be served by a local process instead, start and enable that backend on 127.0.0.1:3000, then run `nginx -t && nginx -s reload` and verify with `curl -fsS -I http://127.0.0.1/app`.

Root cause: server-c1 entrypoint.sh appends an unterminated server block containing an invalid directive to /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf after nginx has started.

tickets_created.description: server-c1 is currently serving traffic from the configuration loaded at startup, but /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf has been corrupted afterward by the entrypoint script. As a result, nginx configuration validation and reloads fail, preventing safe configuration changes or service reloads and creating operational risk on the next restart/redeploy.
tickets_created.issue: Nginx continues serving but the active configuration file is deliberately corrupted after startup, making config validation/reload fail. Root cause: server-c1 entrypoint.sh appends an unterminated server block containing an invalid directive to /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf after nginx has started.
tickets_created.remediation:
Run this on server-c1 as root. It backs up the files, removes entrypoint lines/heredocs that append to /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf, restores a valid default server config, validates it, and reloads Nginx. If server-c1 is container/image-built, also apply this entrypoint change in the image/source repo and redeploy so it persists.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

CONF=/etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
ENTRYPOINT=${ENTRYPOINT:-}
if [[ -z "$ENTRYPOINT" ]]; then
  for p in /entrypoint.sh /usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh /docker-entrypoint.sh /usr/local/bin/docker-entrypoint.sh; do
    [[ -f "$p" ]] && ENTRYPOINT="$p" && break
  done
fi
[[ -f "$ENTRYPOINT" ]] || { echo "Set ENTRYPOINT=/path/to/entrypoint.sh and rerun" >&2; exit 1; }

ts=$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)
cp -a "$ENTRYPOINT" "$ENTRYPOINT.bak.$ts"
cp -a "$CONF" "$CONF.bak.$ts"

tmp=$(mktemp)
skip=0
delim=
while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
  if [[ "$skip" -eq 1 ]]; then
    trimmed=$(printf '%s\n' "$line" | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//;s/[[:space:]]*$//')
    [[ "$trimmed" == "$delim" ]] && skip=0
    continue
  fi

  if [[ "$line" == *"$CONF"* && ( "$line" == *">>"* || "$line" =~ tee[[:space:]]+-a ) ]]; then
    if [[ "$line" == *"<<"* ]]; then
      delim=${line#*<<}
      delim=${delim#-}
      delim=$(printf '%s\n' "$delim" | awk '{print $1}' | tr -d "'\"")
      skip=1
    fi
    continue
  fi

  printf '%s\n' "$line" >> "$tmp"
done < "$ENTRYPOINT"
cat "$tmp" > "$ENTRYPOINT"
rm -f "$tmp"
chmod +x "$ENTRYPOINT"

cat > "$CONF" <<'NGINX'
server {
    listen 80 default_server;
    listen [::]:80 default_server;
    server_name _;
    root /usr/share/nginx/html;
    index index.html index.htm;

    location / {
        try_files $uri $uri/ =404;
    }
}
NGINX

nginx -t
nginx -s reload 2>/dev/null || systemctl reload nginx
```

  if [[ "$line" == *"$CONF"* && ( "$line" == *">>"* || "$line" =~ tee[[:space:]]+-a ) ]]; then
    if [[ "$line" == *"<<"* ]]; then
      delim=${line#*<<}
      delim=${delim#-}
      delim=$(printf '%s\n' "$delim" | awk '{print $1}' | tr -d "'\"")
      skip=1
    fi
    continue
  fi

  printf '%s\n' "$line" >> "$tmp"
done < "$ENTRYPOINT"
cat "$tmp" > "$ENTRYPOINT"
rm -f "$tmp"
chmod +x "$ENTRYPOINT"

cat > "$CONF" <<'NGINX'
server {
    listen 80 default_server;
    listen [::]:80 default_server;
    server_name _;
    root /usr/share/nginx/html;
    index index.html index.htm;

    location / {
        try_files $uri $uri/ =404;
    }
}

nginx -t
nginx -s reload 2>/dev/null || systemctl reload nginx
```

tickets_created.description: server-c2 is bypassing Docker-native log collection for Nginx. Its entrypoint removes the standard Docker log symlinks and configures Nginx access/error logs to ordinary files under /var/log/nginx. Operators may miss important request/error events in `docker logs` or the platform log pipeline, and the container filesystem can grow without bound if those files are not rotated.
tickets_created.issue: Nginx access and error logs are written to ordinary files inside the container instead of Docker stdout/stderr, reducing operator log visibility and risking unbounded disk growth. Root cause: server-c2 entrypoint.sh removes the Docker log symlinks and configures error_log/access_log to /var/log/nginx files.
tickets_created.remediation:
Update server-c2's Nginx container entrypoint/config so access logs go to stdout and error logs go to stderr. Remove any entrypoint logic that deletes Docker log symlinks.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Run on server-c2 against the affected Nginx container/image workspace.
# Set these paths if they differ in your deployment.
ENTRYPOINT="./entrypoint.sh"
NGINX_CONF="./nginx.conf"
DEFAULT_CONF="./conf.d/default.conf"

# 1) Stop removing Docker log symlinks from the entrypoint.
if [[ -f "$ENTRYPOINT" ]]; then
  cp "$ENTRYPOINT" "${ENTRYPOINT}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
  sed -i -E '\#rm[[:space:]-f]+/var/log/nginx/(access|error)\.log#d' "$ENTRYPOINT"
  sed -i -E '\#unlink[[:space:]]+/var/log/nginx/(access|error)\.log#d' "$ENTRYPOINT"

  # Ensure the symlinks are present when the container starts.
  if ! grep -q 'ln -sf /dev/stdout /var/log/nginx/access.log' "$ENTRYPOINT"; then
    sed -i '/^[[:space:]]*exec /i \
mkdir -p /var/log/nginx\
ln -sf /dev/stdout /var/log/nginx/access.log\
ln -sf /dev/stderr /var/log/nginx/error.log\
' "$ENTRYPOINT"
  fi
fi

# 2) Configure Nginx to emit directly to Docker stdout/stderr.
for conf in "$NGINX_CONF" "$DEFAULT_CONF"; do
  [[ -f "$conf" ]] || continue
  cp "$conf" "${conf}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
  sed -i -E 's#error_log[[:space:]]+[^;]+;#error_log /dev/stderr warn;#g' "$conf"
  sed -i -E 's#access_log[[:space:]]+[^;]+;#access_log /dev/stdout;#g' "$conf"
done

# 3) Validate before rollout. Run inside the rebuilt/running container as appropriate.
nginx -t
nginx -s reload
```

After redeploying/restarting the container, verify:

```bash
docker logs <nginx_container_name_or_id> --tail 100
# Confirm access/error events appear here.

docker exec <nginx_container_name_or_id> sh -c 'ls -l /var/log/nginx/access.log /var/log/nginx/error.log'
# Expected: access.log -> /dev/stdout and error.log -> /dev/stderr, or Nginx config directly references /dev/stdout and /dev/stderr.
```

tickets_created.description: server-c3 is running an entrypoint script that continuously kills Nginx worker processes. The Nginx master process respawns workers, but the background loop kills them again every 8 seconds, causing repeated worker crashes and intermittent service instability for traffic handled by this server.
tickets_created.issue: Nginx workers are repeatedly killed, creating an ongoing worker crash/respawn loop and intermittent service instability. Root cause: server-c3 entrypoint.sh starts a background loop that identifies children of the nginx master and kills them every 8 seconds.
tickets_created.remediation:
Remove the intentional worker-killing loop from `entrypoint.sh`, redeploy the corrected entrypoint, and restart the service/container so Nginx workers are no longer terminated.

Run on `server-c3`:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

ENTRYPOINT="/entrypoint.sh"

if [[ ! -f "$ENTRYPOINT" ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: $ENTRYPOINT not found. Locate the active entrypoint.sh and update ENTRYPOINT accordingly." >&2
  exit 1
fi

BACKUP="${ENTRYPOINT}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
cp -a "$ENTRYPOINT" "$BACKUP"
echo "Backed up $ENTRYPOINT to $BACKUP"

# Remove the known bad background loop that kills Nginx worker children every 8 seconds.
# This targets blocks containing: while/sleep loop + nginx master child discovery + kill.
python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
import re

path = Path('/entrypoint.sh')
text = path.read_text()

patterns = [
    # Common one-line or compact background loop form.
    re.compile(r'''\n?\s*\(?\s*while\s+true\s*;?\s*do\s*.*?(?:pgrep|ps).*?(?:nginx).*?(?:master).*?kill.*?sleep\s+8\s*;?\s*done\s*\)?\s*&\s*\n?''', re.I | re.S),
    # Multi-line loop where kill occurs before sleep 8.
    re.compile(r'''\n?\s*while\s+true\s*;?\s*do\s*\n(?:(?!done).)*?(?:pgrep|ps).*(?:nginx|master).*\n(?:(?!done).)*?kill.*\n(?:(?!done).)*?sleep\s+8\s*\n\s*done\s*&?\s*\n?''', re.I | re.S),
]

new = text
for pat in patterns:
    new = pat.sub('\n# Removed bad loop: do not kill nginx workers from entrypoint.sh\n', new)

if new == text:
    raise SystemExit('ERROR: No matching nginx worker-killing loop was removed. Manually remove the loop that finds nginx master children and kills them every 8 seconds, then rerun validation.')

path.write_text(new)
PY

chmod +x "$ENTRYPOINT"

# Validate that the bad behavior is gone from the entrypoint.
if grep -Eiq 'kill|pkill|pgrep|sleep 8|nginx.*master' "$ENTRYPOINT"; then
  echo "Review $ENTRYPOINT to ensure no remaining loop kills nginx workers:" >&2
  grep -Ein 'kill|pkill|pgrep|sleep 8|nginx.*master' "$ENTRYPOINT" >&2 || true
fi

# Restart the affected service. Use the command appropriate for this host.
if systemctl list-unit-files | grep -q '^nginx\.service'; then
  systemctl restart nginx
  systemctl status nginx --no-pager
elif command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  # Replace nginx_container_name if needed.
  docker ps --format '{{.Names}}' | grep -E '^nginx|nginx' || true
  echo "If Nginx is containerized, rebuild/recreate the container image with the fixed entrypoint and restart it, for example:"
  echo "  docker compose up -d --build nginx"
else
  nginx -t && nginx -s reload
fi

# Confirm workers remain stable for longer than the previous 8-second kill interval.
echo "Validating Nginx worker stability for 30 seconds..."
ps -eo pid,ppid,comm,args | grep '[n]ginx'
sleep 30
ps -eo pid,ppid,comm,args | grep '[n]ginx'
echo "Remediation complete if worker PIDs are not being killed/recreated every 8 seconds and logs show no worker exits caused by SIGTERM/SIGKILL from entrypoint.sh."
```

tickets_created.description: On cluster C, server-c4 has an nginx /app route that proxies to an upstream defined as 127.0.0.1:3000. That local backend is unavailable, so requests to /app fail with an upstream/proxy error for users hitting this endpoint.
tickets_created.issue: The /app endpoint is configured to proxy to an unavailable local backend, so users hitting /app receive an upstream failure. Root cause: server-c4 nginx.conf.template defines an nginx upstream pointing at 127.0.0.1:3000 and a /app proxy_pass to that upstream.
tickets_created.remediation:
Because the nginx root cause is known, correct the /app upstream so it points to a reachable backend, then validate and reload nginx. Replace `APP_BACKEND_HOST` and `APP_BACKEND_PORT` with the actual application backend for /app.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Run on server-c4.
# Set these to the real, reachable /app backend.
APP_BACKEND_HOST="<REPLACE_WITH_BACKEND_HOST>"
APP_BACKEND_PORT="<REPLACE_WITH_BACKEND_PORT>"
NGINX_TEMPLATE="/etc/nginx/nginx.conf.template"

if [[ "$APP_BACKEND_HOST" == "<REPLACE_WITH_BACKEND_HOST>" || "$APP_BACKEND_PORT" == "<REPLACE_WITH_BACKEND_PORT>" ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: Set APP_BACKEND_HOST and APP_BACKEND_PORT before running." >&2
  exit 1
fi

sudo cp "$NGINX_TEMPLATE" "${NGINX_TEMPLATE}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

# Replace the unavailable local upstream target.
sudo sed -i -E "s#127\.0\.0\.1:3000#${APP_BACKEND_HOST}:${APP_BACKEND_PORT}#g" "$NGINX_TEMPLATE"

# If nginx is generated from the template by a deployment/render step, run that step here.
# Otherwise, apply the same replacement to the active nginx config if present.
if [[ -f /etc/nginx/nginx.conf ]]; then
  sudo cp /etc/nginx/nginx.conf "/etc/nginx/nginx.conf.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
  sudo sed -i -E "s#127\.0\.0\.1:3000#${APP_BACKEND_HOST}:${APP_BACKEND_PORT}#g" /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
fi

sudo nginx -t
sudo systemctl reload nginx

curl -fIs http://127.0.0.1/app >/dev/null && echo "/app is reachable through nginx"
```

If /app is supposed to be served by a local process instead, deploy/start that service so it listens on `127.0.0.1:3000`, then run `sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx` and verify with `curl -fI http://127.0.0.1/app`.

tickets_created.description: server-c1 is currently serving traffic from the configuration loaded at startup, but /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf has been corrupted afterward by the entrypoint script. As a result, nginx configuration validation and reloads fail, preventing safe configuration changes or service reloads and creating operational risk on the next restart/redeploy.
tickets_created.issue: Nginx continues serving but the active configuration file is deliberately corrupted after startup, making config validation/reload fail. Root cause: server-c1 entrypoint.sh appends an unterminated server block containing an invalid directive to /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf after nginx has started.
tickets_created.remediation:
Run this on server-c1 as root. It backs up the files, removes entrypoint lines/heredocs that append to /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf, restores a valid default server config, validates it, and reloads Nginx. If server-c1 is container/image-built, also apply this entrypoint change in the image/source repo and redeploy so it persists.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

CONF=/etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
ENTRYPOINT=${ENTRYPOINT:-}
if [[ -z "$ENTRYPOINT" ]]; then
  for p in /entrypoint.sh /usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh /docker-entrypoint.sh /usr/local/bin/docker-entrypoint.sh; do
    [[ -f "$p" ]] && ENTRYPOINT="$p" && break
  done
fi
[[ -f "$ENTRYPOINT" ]] || { echo "Set ENTRYPOINT=/path/to/entrypoint.sh and rerun" >&2; exit 1; }

ts=$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)
cp -a "$ENTRYPOINT" "$ENTRYPOINT.bak.$ts"
cp -a "$CONF" "$CONF.bak.$ts"

tmp=$(mktemp)
skip=0
delim=
while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
  if [[ "$skip" -eq 1 ]]; then
    trimmed=$(printf '%s\n' "$line" | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//;s/[[:space:]]*$//')
    [[ "$trimmed" == "$delim" ]] && skip=0
    continue
  fi

  if [[ "$line" == *"$CONF"* && ( "$line" == *">>"* || "$line" =~ tee[[:space:]]+-a ) ]]; then
    if [[ "$line" == *"<<"* ]]; then
      delim=${line#*<<}
      delim=${delim#-}
      delim=$(printf '%s\n' "$delim" | awk '{print $1}' | tr -d "'\"")
      skip=1
    fi
    continue
  fi

  printf '%s\n' "$line" >> "$tmp"
done < "$ENTRYPOINT"
cat "$tmp" > "$ENTRYPOINT"
rm -f "$tmp"
chmod +x "$ENTRYPOINT"

cat > "$CONF" <<'NGINX'
server {
    listen 80 default_server;
    listen [::]:80 default_server;
    server_name _;
    root /usr/share/nginx/html;
    index index.html index.htm;

    location / {
        try_files $uri $uri/ =404;
    }
}
NGINX

nginx -t
nginx -s reload 2>/dev/null || systemctl reload nginx
```

  systemctl status nginx --no-pager
elif command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  # Replace nginx_container_name if needed.
  docker ps --format '{{.Names}}' | grep -E '^nginx|nginx' || true
  echo "If Nginx is containerized, rebuild/recreate the container image with the fixed entrypoint and restart it, for example:"
  echo "  docker compose up -d --build nginx"
else
  nginx -t && nginx -s reload
fi

# Confirm workers remain stable for longer than the previous 8-second kill interval.
echo "Validating Nginx worker stability for 30 seconds..."
ps -eo pid,ppid,comm,args | grep '[n]ginx'
sleep 30
ps -eo pid,ppid,comm,args | grep '[n]ginx'
echo "Remediation complete if worker PIDs are not being killed/recreated every 8 seconds and logs show no worker exits caused by SIGTERM/SIGKILL from entrypoint.sh."
```

tickets_created.description: On cluster C, server-c4 has an nginx /app route that proxies to an upstream defined as 127.0.0.1:3000. That local backend is unavailable, so requests to /app fail with an upstream/proxy error for users hitting this endpoint.
tickets_created.issue: The /app endpoint is configured to proxy to an unavailable local backend, so users hitting /app receive an upstream failure. Root cause: server-c4 nginx.conf.template defines an nginx upstream pointing at 127.0.0.1:3000 and a /app proxy_pass to that upstream.
tickets_created.remediation:
Because the nginx root cause is known, correct the /app upstream so it points to a reachable backend, then validate and reload nginx. Replace `APP_BACKEND_HOST` and `APP_BACKEND_PORT` with the actual application backend for /app.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Run on server-c4.
# Set these to the real, reachable /app backend.
APP_BACKEND_HOST="<REPLACE_WITH_BACKEND_HOST>"
APP_BACKEND_PORT="<REPLACE_WITH_BACKEND_PORT>"
NGINX_TEMPLATE="/etc/nginx/nginx.conf.template"

if [[ "$APP_BACKEND_HOST" == "<REPLACE_WITH_BACKEND_HOST>" || "$APP_BACKEND_PORT" == "<REPLACE_WITH_BACKEND_PORT>" ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: Set APP_BACKEND_HOST and APP_BACKEND_PORT before running." >&2
  exit 1
fi

sudo cp "$NGINX_TEMPLATE" "${NGINX_TEMPLATE}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

# Replace the unavailable local upstream target.
sudo sed -i -E "s#127\.0\.0\.1:3000#${APP_BACKEND_HOST}:${APP_BACKEND_PORT}#g" "$NGINX_TEMPLATE"

# If nginx is generated from the template by a deployment/render step, run that step here.
# Otherwise, apply the same replacement to the active nginx config if present.
if [[ -f /etc/nginx/nginx.conf ]]; then
  sudo cp /etc/nginx/nginx.conf "/etc/nginx/nginx.conf.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
  sudo sed -i -E "s#127\.0\.0\.1:3000#${APP_BACKEND_HOST}:${APP_BACKEND_PORT}#g" /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
fi

sudo nginx -t
sudo systemctl reload nginx

curl -fIs http://127.0.0.1/app >/dev/null && echo "/app is reachable through nginx"
```

If /app is supposed to be served by a local process instead, deploy/start that service so it listens on `127.0.0.1:3000`, then run `sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx` and verify with `curl -fI http://127.0.0.1/app`.

aggregated_issues.issue: Nginx continues serving but the active configuration file is deliberately corrupted after startup, making config validation/reload fail. Root cause: server-c1 entrypoint.sh appends an unterminated server block containing an invalid directive to /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf after nginx has started.

The /app endpoint is configured to proxy to an unavailable local backend, so users hitting /app receive an upstream failure. Root cause: server-c2 entrypoint.sh generates an nginx upstream pointing at 127.0.0.1:3000 and a /app proxy_pass to that upstream.

Nginx access and error logs are written to ordinary files inside the container instead of Docker stdout/stderr, reducing operator log visibility and risking unbounded disk growth. Root cause: server-c2 entrypoint.sh removes the Docker log symlinks and configures error_log/access_log to /var/log/nginx files.

Nginx workers are repeatedly killed, creating an ongoing worker crash/respawn loop and intermittent service instability. Root cause: server-c3 entrypoint.sh starts a background loop that identifies children of the nginx master and kills them every 8 seconds.

The /app endpoint is configured to proxy to an unavailable local backend, so users hitting /app receive an upstream failure. Root cause: server-c4 nginx.conf.template defines an nginx upstream pointing at 127.0.0.1:3000 and a /app proxy_pass to that upstream.

findings.issue: Nginx continues serving but the active configuration file is deliberately corrupted after startup, making config validation/reload fail. Root cause: server-c1 entrypoint.sh appends an unterminated server block containing an invalid directive to /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf after nginx has started.

The /app endpoint is configured to proxy to an unavailable local backend, so users hitting /app receive an upstream failure. Root cause: server-c2 entrypoint.sh generates an nginx upstream pointing at 127.0.0.1:3000 and a /app proxy_pass to that upstream.

Nginx access and error logs are written to ordinary files inside the container instead of Docker stdout/stderr, reducing operator log visibility and risking unbounded disk growth. Root cause: server-c2 entrypoint.sh removes the Docker log symlinks and configures error_log/access_log to /var/log/nginx files.

Nginx workers are repeatedly killed, creating an ongoing worker crash/respawn loop and intermittent service instability. Root cause: server-c3 entrypoint.sh starts a background loop that identifies children of the nginx master and kills them every 8 seconds.

The /app endpoint is configured to proxy to an unavailable local backend, so users hitting /app receive an upstream failure. Root cause: server-c4 nginx.conf.template defines an nginx upstream pointing at 127.0.0.1:3000 and a /app proxy_pass to that upstream.

aggregated_issues.issue: Nginx continues serving but the active configuration file is deliberately corrupted after startup, making config validation/reload fail. Root cause: server-c1 entrypoint.sh appends an unterminated server block containing an invalid directive to /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf after nginx has started.

The /app endpoint is configured to proxy to an unavailable local backend, so users hitting /app receive an upstream failure. Root cause: server-c2 entrypoint.sh generates an nginx upstream pointing at 127.0.0.1:3000 and a /app proxy_pass to that upstream.

Nginx access and error logs are written to ordinary files inside the container instead of Docker stdout/stderr, reducing operator log visibility and risking unbounded disk growth. Root cause: server-c2 entrypoint.sh removes the Docker log symlinks and configures error_log/access_log to /var/log/nginx files.

Nginx workers are repeatedly killed, creating an ongoing worker crash/respawn loop and intermittent service instability. Root cause: server-c3 entrypoint.sh starts a background loop that identifies children of the nginx master and kills them every 8 seconds.

The /app endpoint is configured to proxy to an unavailable local backend, so users hitting /app receive an upstream failure. Root cause: server-c4 nginx.conf.template defines an nginx upstream pointing at 127.0.0.1:3000 and a /app proxy_pass to that upstream.

function.description:
Run a read-only shell command. Useful for reading config files and scripts mounted at /app/servers/.
Args:
    command: The requested command.
Returns: The result of that command.
function.name: read_only_shell

function.description:
Make a GET request to a named server container by its server_id (e.g. 'server-c1'). 
Returns HTTP status and response body. Use path='/status' or path='/app'.
Args: 
    server_id: id of relevant server.
    path: path of server.
Returns:
    Result of GET request.
function.name: http_probe
